title: Control Stack PASS: cymatics and seismology share orbit trajectory singularity→satellite→cosmos under the same compiler law
_fixture_purpose: Canonical PASS cert for the control stack. Certifies that QA_PLAN_CONTROL_COMPILER_CERT.v1 [106] is genuinely domain-generic by comparing two physical instantiations: (1) cymatics [105]: flat→stripes→hexagons via [increase_amplitude, set_frequency], orbit singularity→satellite→cosmos, k=2; (2) seismology [110]: quiet→p_wave→surface_wave via [increase_gain, apply_lowpass], same orbit trajectory, k=2. Different physics (Faraday instability vs. elastic wave propagation), different moves, same orbit trajectory and path length — the compiler law is structural, not domain-specific.

domain_traces.notes: Faraday instability. flat=sub-threshold (fixed point, singularity). stripes=threshold-crossing symmetry break (satellite orbit). hexagons=organized hexagonal pattern (cosmos orbit). Source: qa_cymatics/fixtures/control_cert_pass_hexagon_with_kernel_ref.json

Elastic wave propagation. quiet=quiescent (fixed point, singularity). p_wave=primary compressional wave (structured, satellite orbit). surface_wave=organized long-period propagation (cosmos orbit). Source: qa_seismic_control/fixtures/seismic_control_pass_surface_wave.json

cross_domain_claim.notes: The orbit trajectory singularity→satellite→cosmos and path_length_k=2 are invariants of the compiler law, not artifacts of a particular physical domain. Cymatics and seismology are distinct physical systems (fluid instability vs. elastic solid), yet both instantiate the same QA trajectory under the same kernel-governed generator algebra. The compiler law governs the abstract orbit structure; the physical domain supplies only the concrete state labels and generator implementations.
